template_text: For any KYC update request, contact your bank or financial institution through official channels before taking action.
Do not share account login details, card information, PIN, password, OTP, or KYC documents with unknown persons.
notes: Use as genuine anchor against fake KYC requests.

template_text: If financial cyber fraud occurs, immediately report through cybercrime.gov.in or helpline 1930.
Do not install unauthorized or unverified apps from links sent in KYC update messages.
Your UPI PIN authorizes bank transactions. Do not share it with anyone.
A collect request asks the payer to approve or decline a request in the UPI app.
Unknown money requests can be declined and blocked from the Approve to Pay section.
For IPO UPI mandates, check the verified merchant indication and match bid details before approving.
After a successful UPI payment, the app shows status and the bank sends transaction confirmation.
Open BHIM, review pending requests, and approve only requests you recognize.
To report cyber incidents, email report.phishing@sbi.co.in or call cybercrime helpline 1930.
SBI never asks for user ID, password, card number, PIN, password, or CVV.
A PIN is needed only for transferring money, not for receiving money.
Cyber criminals may call customers and ask them to click a link to update KYC; verify instead.
No money should be sent because of threats made on video calls by people claiming to be officers.
Banks do not request KYC updates through random links or calls; use the official website or branch.
Do not share customer ID, login password, OTP, or card details over calls, messages, or emails.
There is no legal process called digital arrest; stay calm and report suspicious calls.
A QR code is scanned to send money, not to receive a payment.
Type the bank URL yourself instead of clicking links received by email or text.
Banks never ask for login password, transaction password, OTP, or URN by email or phone.
Do not click links, download files, or open attachments from unknown senders.
Forward suspicious phishing emails claiming to be ICICI Bank to antiphishing@icicibank.com.
ICICI Bank will never call to ask for PIN or OTP or say your card is blocked over the phone.
Cross-check online payments with SMS or email receipts and report suspicious activity to the bank.
Use Internet Banking only through the bank official website or mobile app.
Do not share OTPs, passwords, or sensitive data over calls claiming to be from the bank.
Axis Bank will not ask for personal or banking information by phone, email, or SMS.
While accepting a collection request, ensure the correct recipient has been approved.
Do not share OTP, CVV, PIN, password, or account details with anyone claiming urgent verification.
notes: Placeholder until a specific BoB safe-banking URL is manually verified.

template_text: Use official branch, website, or app channels for KYC service requests.
notes: Placeholder; replace after manual BoB page confirmation.

template_text: Report financial cyber fraud quickly through cybercrime.gov.in or helpline 1930.
No government agency conducts investigation over phone or video call.
Stop, think, then take action: report to 1930 or cybercrime.gov.in and preserve evidence.
Do not provide personal information after unsolicited courier calls or messages; report suspicious contact.
notes: Reported in news; verify company page before citing as direct FedEx source.
